title: Stmt and Script are bare Vec type aliases
description: form.rs: pub type Stmt = Vec<Form> and pub type Script = Vec<Stmt> are bare type aliases, not newtypes. This means callers can freely push arbitrary Forms into a Stmt or Stmts into a Script with no type boundary. More concretely, it is impossible to implement Iterator, Display, or From<Script> on Script because they are Vec — you cannot implement foreign traits on foreign types. Newtypes (struct Stmt(Vec<Form>); struct Script(Vec<Stmt>);) with Deref impls would allow those trait impls, enable API evolution without breaking callers, and make the public API surface self-documenting. The evaluator already treats them as abstract sequences; the only thing lost is that the form parser builds them with .push() which would remain private.
